paper_title: The effects of trade on national productivity -A stimulant or its handmaiden: An empirical evidence from ASEAN nations

entities.description: The primary author of the research paper.

entities.id: College of Economics, Can Tho University
entities.description: The academic institution where the author is affiliated.
The main field of study concerning the exchange of goods and services between nations.
The broader scientific field investigating production, consumption, and transfer of wealth.
The core concept being investigated as a stimulant or handmaiden of growth.
The research problem proxied by economic growth rate in ASEAN nations.
Foreign direct investment used as a variable influencing growth.
The degree to which an economy allows or encourages international trade.
The theory that trade acts as a translator of growth rather than a direct driver.
A statistical method used to regress the causal relationship between trade and growth while bypassing autocorrelation and heteroskedasticity.
A multivariate statistical analysis technique used to measure mediating effects.
A resampling method used within the SEM framework.
The source of data for the 10-year span (2012-2021) study of ASEAN nations.
A secondary dataset mentioned in literature review regarding export-led growth.
Indicator of homoskedasticity in the regression model.
Metric measuring multicollinearity among variables.
Specific measurement of trade openness for Singapore in 2012.
The shift in coefficient after inducing for Trade Incentive.
Metric for FDI attraction performance in 2021.

entities.id: Export from FDI sectors in Vietnam (76.3%)
entities.description: The proportion of total exports attributed to foreign-invested sectors.
